reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio with perfect content accuracy, matching the text '同学们请注意，这项实验非常关键。现在请大家立刻开始操作，务必在五分钟内完成！'. regarding the core feature (Speed Adjustment), the model successfully executes the instruction. Segment 1 (00:00 - 00:04) is delivered at a moderate, steady pace (approx. 3.7 characters/second), serving as the 'slow' start relative to the second half. Segment 2 (00:04 - 00:08) shows a distinct and sudden increase in speech rate (approx. 5.4 characters/second) for the text '现在请大家立刻开始操作，务必在五分钟内完成！', effectively conveying urgency. The transition is smooth yet noticeable, fulfilling the requirement of 'suddenly speed up'. The audio is fluent and natural.
reasoning_model_2: The model accurately speaks the content script, although the articulation is heavily stylized with a breathless, dramatic tone. Regarding the core feature (Speed Adjustment), the model fails to meet the requirements fully. Segment 1 (00:00 - 00:05) is indeed delivered at a slow speed with heavy pauses (approx. 2.6 characters/second). However, for Segment 2 (00:05 - 00:11), the model fails to 'suddenly speed up to fast'. Instead, it continues at a similar slow, plodding pace (approx. 3.1 characters/second) with significant pauses between words (e.g., '现在...请大家...'). While the emotional intensity changes, the objective speech rate does not become 'fast' as requested, failing the second half of the instruction.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It correctly interprets the dynamic adjustment command by establishing a baseline speed and then visibly accelerating for the second sentence, creating the intended contrast and urgency. Model 2 starts with a distinct slow speed but fails to transition to a fast speed, remaining slow throughout the entire clip. Therefore, Model 1 follows the paralinguistic instruction significantly better.
